text: The Great Summer Quest of Prisha and Samaira

The school bell gave one final, happy ring! Prisha and Samaira jumped for joy, clutching their KG-I diplomas. They had finished their exams and learned so many things, but now the best part of the year was starting. 'It’s summer!' Prisha cheered, throwing her sun hat into the air. Samaira caught it with a giggle, her eyes sparkling with excitement.

Outside the school gates, a familiar face was waiting with a mischievous grin. It was Aunt Saka! She was wearing her favorite sunglasses and holding a rolled-up piece of parchment. 'Congratulations, my favorite explorers!' Saka said, unrolling the paper to reveal a hand-drawn map of the city. 'The Summer Quest begins today. Are you ready for an adventure?'

The first stop on the map was home to prepare their supplies. Prisha and Saka worked together to pack the ultimate adventure bag. They stuffed in fluffy towels, bright yellow flippers, and a bag of crunchy star-shaped crackers. Saka showed Prisha how to fold her swimsuit into a tiny ball to make more room. 'A good explorer is always prepared!' Saka laughed.

The map led them straight to the Sparkling Coast. The sand was as white as sugar and the water was a perfect, clear blue. Prisha and Samaira didn't wait a second—they kicked off their sandals and raced toward the waves. The ocean felt cool and ticklish on their toes as they splashed each other, sending diamonds of water flying into the air.

While exploring the tide pools, Samaira met her friend Viraj. They both wore big sun hats and carried little buckets. 'Look what I found!' Viraj shouted, pointing to a crevice in the rocks. Together, they discovered a shell that shimmered like a rainbow. Samaira held it up to the sun, watching the colors dance. 'It’s a treasure from the Deep Sea Kingdom!' she whispered.

'Adventurers need fuel!' Saka announced. She led the twins to a small shack painted like a giant strawberry. Saka and Prisha each held a towering cone with three scoops of different colored ice cream. The sun was hot, and the ice cream started to melt, dripping down their hands. They raced to see who could lick the drips the fastest.

As the sun began to set, the adventure moved to the backyard. It was time for a secret campout! Samaira helped her Mom, Shweta, pull the heavy green tent poles through the fabric. 'It’s like building a castle,' Shweta said with a wink. Samaira pulled the ropes tight, making sure their nylon home was sturdy enough for a night under the stars.

When the stars finally came out, Saka brought out a long, silver telescope. She and Samaira took turns peeking through the lens. 'Look, Samaira! That’s the Great Bear,' Saka said, pointing to the sky. Samaira gasped as she saw the stars connect like dots in a giant, glowing drawing. The moon looked so close she felt like she could touch it.

The next evening, the whole neighborhood gathered for the Summer Festival. The air smelled like grilled corn and sweet candy. Prisha and her friend Rani held glowing sparklers, swirling them around to write their names in the air. The golden sparks danced around them like tiny fireflies, making the night feel truly magical.

After a long week of sun, sand, and stars, Prisha and Samaira finally climbed into their cozy bunk beds. They were tired, but their hearts were full of happy memories. 'Saka said there's a new map for tomorrow,' Prisha whispered from the top bunk. Samaira nodded sleepily from below. 'Summer is the best adventure of all,' she murmured as they drifted off to dream.
